name: Corruption Perceptions Index
data_exists: YES — TI CPI 2025 already includes Brunei (score 63/100)
agency: Prime Minister's Office / Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB)
fix_desc: Brunei CPI 2025 now scores 63/100 (rank 31). TI excluded Brunei from earlier GTCI cycle due to data submission gaps. ACB should establish a formal annual engagement with Transparency International to ensure Brunei is consistently included and scored in CPI. Data already exists — it is a perception survey that TI now covers.

name: R&D Expenditure
data_exists: PARTIAL — Some R&D data exists; comprehensive reporting to UNESCO needed
source: UNESCO Institute for Statistics, WIPO GII, OECD MSTI
agency: Ministry of Finance & Economy / DST Research
fix_desc: R&D Expenditure ~0.1% of GDP: Genuinely low — not a data gap. Requires multi-year budget commitment. Short-term: ensure all R&D spending (UBD grants, BRUDIRECT innovation funds, O&G sector R&D) is comprehensively reported to UNESCO/OECD MSTI database. Some R&D may be occurring but not captured in official statistics.
timeline: Short-term reporting fix: 3-6 months; structural improvement: 5-10 years

name: Internet access in schools
fix_category: Missing – Ministry Not Reporting to WEF
data_exists: YES — MOE has school ICT connectivity data (OGN (One Government Network) connects all government schools via EGNC)
agency: Ministry of Education (MOE)
fix_desc: Internet in Schools: Score=0 because Brunei business leaders did not respond to WEF EOS question about internet in schools, and no hard data was submitted. MOE TVET and Digital Infrastructure Division has full data on OGN (One Government Network, managed by EGNC — egnc.gov.bn) connectivity in all schools. Submit this to WEF and ensure EOS respondents are briefed.

name: Household financial resilience
data_exists: YES — BDCB has comprehensive national financial inclusion data
agency: Ministry of Finance & Economy / BDCB
fix_desc: Global Findex 2024 does not include Brunei. BDCB (Brunei Darussalam Central Bank, formerly AMBD, renamed June 2021, bdcb.gov.bn) has national financial inclusion data showing >90% bank account ownership among Bruneian nationals. Ministry of Finance & Economy should formally request World Bank to include Brunei in Global Findex 2027. This is a pure data submission issue — Brunei's actual financial inclusion is strong. Expected score if included: 70-85/100.

name: Personal safety
fix_category: Missing – Not in Social Progress Index
data_exists: YES — RBPF publishes annual crime statistics; national safety data is comprehensive
agency: Royal Brunei Police Force / Ministry of Home Affairs
fix_desc: Social Progress Index (Personal Safety) does not include Brunei. Brunei has one of the lowest crime rates in Southeast Asia. RBPF crime statistics are published annually and show extremely low rates of assault, theft, homicide, and violent crime. This is a missed opportunity — Brunei's actual personal safety performance is excellent and would likely score 70-85/100 if submitted to Social Progress Imperative.

name: Researchers, Full-time equivalent
data_exists: PARTIAL — Researcher headcount data exists; completeness of UNESCO submission uncertain
source: Global Innovation Index Databas, WIPO 2025. UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS) OECD, Main Science and Technology Indicators (MSTI) Database Ibero-American and Inter-American Network of Science and Technology Indicators (RICYT)
agency: Ministry of Education / UBD / UTB / Brunei Research Council (proposed)
fix_desc: Researcher Density (4.82): Genuinely low — not a data gap. Requires long-term investment in research careers, competitive researcher salaries, and dedicated R&D institutes. However, ensure all existing researchers (UBD, UTB, UNISSA, government labs) are comprehensively reported to UNESCO UIS. Under-reporting may be inflating the gap.
timeline: Short-term: improve UNESCO reporting (3-6 months); structural: 5-10 years

name: ICT Services Exports
data_exists: PARTIAL — WTO data available; comprehensive BOP reporting of ICT services needed
source: Global Innovation Index Database, WIPO 2025 WTO nd UN Conference on Trade & Development, Trade in Commercial Services Database
agency: MoFE / BEDB / MTIC
fix_desc: ICT Services Exports (2.44): Genuinely low — reflects actual economic structure. Requires deliberate ICT export strategy targeting ASEAN digital services markets. However, ensure ALL ICT-related service exports (fintech, cybersecurity consulting, IT services) are comprehensively captured in WTO/BOP statistics.
timeline: Short-term: improve BOP reporting (3-6 months); structural: 5-10 years

name: High-Value Exports
data_exists: PARTIAL — Export data available; proper sector classification in UN Comtrade needed
source: UN Comtrade / World Bank WDI / OECD classification
agency: BEDB / Ministry of Finance & Economy
fix_desc: High-Value Exports (1.53): Genuinely low — reflects oil dependency. Economic diversification is the only long-term fix. Short-term: ensure halal food, cosmetics, and pharmaceutical exports are classified correctly under OECD high-tech export categories in UN Comtrade submissions.
timeline: Short-term: reclassification review (3-6 months); structural: 5-10+ years

name: New Business Density
fix_category: Missing – Data Not Submitted to World Bank
data_exists: YES — ROCBN has complete new business registration data for 2023 and 2024
agency: ROCBN (Registry of Companies & Business Names) / MoFE
fix_desc: New Business Density: Score=0 because Brunei did not submit new business registration data to World Bank Entrepreneurship Database before GTCI 2025 data cutoff. ROCBN registered 6,570 new enterprises in 2023 and ~7,200 in 2024. Submitting this data to the World Bank Entrepreneurship Database is the single most impactful easy fix available. Estimated score after submission: 40-60/100. This could lift Brunei 15-20 ranks in GTCI overall.
timeline: IMMEDIATE — data can be submitted within days

name: Enterprise Software
data_exists: NO — Structural low adoption; policy fix required
source: Statista Technology Market Outlook, World Bank WDI
agency: MTIC / Darussalam Enterprise / AITI
fix_desc: Enterprise Software (11.86): Genuinely low adoption — not a data gap. Requires policy intervention: national digitalisation grants, SME software adoption subsidies, and mandatory ERP adoption for government-linked companies. Darussalam Enterprise's Digital Transformation programme is addressing this but impact takes 2-3 years to show in data.

name: Cloud Computing
data_exists: NO — Structural low adoption; policy and investment required
source: Statista Technology Market Outlook, World Bank WDI
agency: MTIC / AITI / Government Chief Information Officer
fix_desc: Cloud Computing (4.79): Genuinely low — not a data gap. Cloud-First Policy needed. Government should mandate cloud migration for non-sensitive workloads, provide SME cloud adoption grants, and develop sovereign cloud framework. DE25/AITI initiative includes cloud acceleration targets.

name: Firms with website
data_exists: PARTIAL — to enquire government agency that collects business climate data; full WBES survey needed
agency: Ministry of Finance & Economy / Darussalam Enterprise
fix_desc: World Bank Enterprise Surveys (WBES) last included Brunei in 2015. Score=0 reflects data absence — not poor business performance. Brunei businesses are sophisticated. Ministry of Finance & Economy should formally request World Bank WBES team to include Brunei in the 2025/2026 survey cycle. Estimated cost: minimal (World Bank funds surveys). Potential score gain: 30-50 pts.
timeline: 3-6 months to request inclusion; survey cycle 6-12 months

name: Prevalence of training in firms
data_exists: PARTIAL — to enquire government agency that collects enterprise training data; WBES inclusion needed for global benchmark
agency: Ministry of Finance & Economy / Darussalam Enterprise
fix_desc: World Bank Enterprise Surveys — same as 1.3.7. Brunei not surveyed since 2015. Both 1.3.7 and 3.2.2 are sourced from WBES. A single request to be included in the survey resolves both indicators simultaneously. This is a two-for-one easy fix — one engagement with World Bank resolves two GTCI zero scores.
timeline: 3-6 months to request; 6-12 months for survey

name: Protect against future disasters
fix_category: Missing – Not in World Risk Poll
data_exists: YES — SHENA should have comprehensive workplace safety data nationally
agency: Ministry of Home Affairs / SHENA
fix_desc: World Risk Poll (LRF) 2024 does not include Brunei. Brunei is empirically one of the safest countries in Southeast Asia. SHENA has detailed workplace safety statistics (accident rates, fatality rates) that would support a high score. Engage Lloyd's Register Foundation to include Brunei in WRP 2027. Alternatively, submit SHENA national workplace safety data for consideration. Expected score: 60-80+/100 based on actual safety record.

name: Personal rights
fix_category: Missing – Not in Social Progress Index
data_exists: PARTIAL — Some data available from national sources; SPI uses composite of publicly available datasets
agency: Prime Minister's Office / Attorney General's Chambers
fix_desc: Social Progress Index (Personal Rights) does not include Brunei. Social Progress Imperative should be engaged to include Brunei based on available governance and rights data. While Brunei's political rights score may be moderate (no multiparty elections), personal liberties (property rights, freedom of movement, freedom of religion) are generally respected. Proactive engagement with SPI could secure a score of 40-60/100.

name: Labour Productivity per Employee
data_exists: YES — DEPS has full national accounts (GDP) and LFS (employment) data
source: The Conference Board, Total Economy Database
agency: Department of Economic Planning & Statistics (DEPS)
fix_desc: Conference Board Total Economy Database (TED) — Brunei not included. DEPS has all national accounts data required to compute labour productivity (GDP per employed person). DEPS should contact The Conference Board directly to submit Brunei's national accounts data (GDP series, employment series) for inclusion in TED. This is a pure data provision exercise — no surveys needed.

name: Software Development
data_exists: PARTIAL — Developer activity exists; open-source contribution culture needed
source: GitHub via WIPO GII 2025 / old methodology UN, Department of Economic and Social Affairs, Population Division, World Population Prospects 2024
agency: MTIC / AITI / AITI / StartUp Brunei
fix_desc: Software Development (3.02): Genuinely low. GitHub commit activity reflects actual developer community size. Growing through AITI coding bootcamps, BruHack hackathons, and StartUp Brunei ecosystem. Ensure Bruneian developers are encouraged to publish code openly on GitHub — many government and private projects may be on private repositories.

name: AI skills migration
data_exists: PARTIAL — LinkedIn data exists but not analysed for Brunei at national level
agency: Ministry of Transport & Infocommunications (MTIC) / AITI
fix_desc: AI Skills Migration: Brunei not in LinkedIn Economic Graph dataset. MTIC/AITI should engage LinkedIn Economic Graph team directly to ensure Brunei data is captured. Growing Brunei LinkedIn user base (~100K+ users) may already be sufficient for LinkedIn to compute an AI skills score. Formal partnership request to LinkedIn needed.

name: Tolerance of immigrant
data_exists: PARTIAL — National surveys exist but not Gallup-compatible
agency: Ministry of Home Affairs / Department of Statistics
fix_desc: Gallup Tolerance of Immigrants: Brunei has ~31% migrant population — actual tolerance attitudes are unknown in global benchmarks because Gallup does not survey Brunei. Department of Statistics could commission a Gallup-compatible national perception survey annually and submit aggregated results. Alternatively, request Gallup include Brunei in their rolling survey programme.

name: Business Masters Education
fix_category: Structural – No Ranked Business School (Score=0 normalised floor)
data_exists: NO — Requires international partnership and sustained research investment
source: QS Global MBA and Business Masters Rankings 2025
agency: Ministry of Education / UBD
fix_desc: Business Masters Education: Score=0.00 is GTCI normalisation floor — Brunei is ranked 64/135 globally, meaning it IS scored (just at the lowest bracket). This is NOT missing data. Brunei has no MBA programme in QS Global MBA Rankings. UBDSBE offers MBA but lacks research output and international faculty required for QS ranking threshold. Path to improvement: pursue AACSB accreditation (accreditation@aacsb.edu), establish joint-degree MBA with a QS top-100 business school (NUS Business School: mba.admissions@nus.edu.sg or Melbourne Business School), and build research output of 30+ ABS-3 journal articles/year.

name: Employee Wellbeing
fix_category: Missing – Not in Gallup Wellbeing Survey
data_exists: PARTIAL — National Life Satisfaction surveys exist but not Gallup-format
agency: Department of Statistics (DEPS) / Ministry of Finance
fix_desc: Gallup World Poll Employee Wellbeing: Brunei not surveyed. National wellbeing likely high based on quality of life indicators. Department of Statistics should commission an annual National Wellbeing Survey using Gallup-compatible methodology, publish results, and coordinate with INSEAD GTCI team for data submission. Cost: ~BND 50,000-100,000 annually for a proper national survey.

name: AI Talent Concentration
data_exists: PARTIAL — AITI digital skills programme data exists; LinkedIn coverage of Brunei AI talent growing
source: LinkedIn Economic Graph OECD AI Policy Observatory
agency: MTIC / AITI / AITI Digital Skills Programme Office
fix_desc: AI Talent Concentration (LinkedIn/OECD): New indicator in GTCI 2025 — Brunei not covered. Digital Economy Masterplan 2025 (DE25) 2025-2030 is building AI talent pipelines. AITI should engage LinkedIn Economic Graph and OECD AI Policy Observatory to ensure Brunei AI workforce data is captured. As AI upskilling programs mature under DE25/AITI, this score will naturally improve.

name: Intellectual Property Receipts
fix_category: Structural – Near-Zero IP Receipts (Score=0 normalised floor)
data_exists: NO — Brunei genuinely has no IP receipts currently
source: IMF Balance of Payments Statistics / WTO Trade Database
agency: Attorney General's Chambers (AGC) / Brunei Intellectual Property Office (BruIPO)
fix_desc: Intellectual Property Receipts: Score=0.00 is GTCI normalisation floor — Brunei is ranked 121/135 globally, meaning it IS scored. This is NOT missing data. IMF Balance of Payments 2024 confirms Brunei earns near-zero IP licensing receipts from abroad. Brunei Intellectual Property Office (BruIPO, established 2012 under Attorney General's Chambers) has extremely low patent filing rates. Actions: introduce Patent Box tax incentive regime (coordinate with MoFE); provide BruIPO commercialisation support for UBD/UTB research outputs; engage WIPO (wipo.int) on technical assistance for IP creation. Structural 5–10 year fix.
